name: sqlalchemy
summary: The Python SQL toolkit and Object Relational Mapper

story.problem: Writing raw SQL strings in Python is fragile: string formatting opens SQL injection vulnerabilities, different databases have different dialects, and mapping query results to Python objects is repetitive boilerplate every team reimplements.
story.mental_model: SQLAlchemy has two layers. The Core is a SQL expression language — you build SQL programmatically using Python objects instead of strings. The ORM maps database rows to Python classes, so you think in objects while SQLAlchemy generates the SQL. Use Core for complex queries; ORM for CRUD on models.
story.when_to_use: Any Python app that talks to a relational database: Flask/FastAPI backends, data pipelines, ETL scripts. Works with SQLite, PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle, and more through a unified API.
story.when_not_to_use: If you only need SQLite for local storage, the stdlib sqlite3 module is sufficient. NoSQL databases have their own clients. For simple async apps, consider databases or SQLModel (built on SQLAlchemy 2 + Pydantic).

alternatives.name: tortoise-orm
alternatives.reason: Async-first ORM with Django-inspired API — great for FastAPI

alternatives.name: peewee
alternatives.reason: Lightweight ORM with a simple API — fewer features, less complexity

alternatives.name: django ORM
alternatives.reason: Tightly integrated with Django — only use if you're in the Django ecosystem

nodes.label: create_engine()
nodes.summary: Create a database connection pool. The starting point for all SQLAlchemy usage.

nodes.label: Session
nodes.summary: Unit of work for ORM operations. Add, query, and commit changes within a session.

nodes.label: DeclarativeBase
nodes.summary: Base class for ORM models. Subclass it to define a mapped table.

nodes.label: Column / mapped_column()
nodes.summary: Define a column in an ORM model. Specify type, constraints, defaults.

nodes.label: select()
nodes.summary: Build a SELECT query using the Core expression language. Use with session.execute().

nodes.label: relationship()
nodes.summary: Define a foreign-key relationship between models. Enables obj.children access.

nodes.label: Mapped[T]
nodes.summary: Type annotation for ORM columns in SQLAlchemy 2.x. Replaces Column() for type safety.

tasks.title: Connect to SQLite and create a table

steps.label: Define model
steps.code:
from sqlalchemy import create_engine
from sqlalchemy.orm import DeclarativeBase, Mapped, mapped_column

engine = create_engine('sqlite:///app.db')

class Base(DeclarativeBase):
    pass

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'
    id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(primary_key=True)
    name: Mapped[str]
    email: Mapped[str]

steps.label: Session setup
steps.code:
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

with Session(engine) as session:

steps.label: Read
steps.code:
from sqlalchemy import select
    stmt = select(User).where(User.name == 'Alice')
    user = session.scalars(stmt).first()

steps.label: Define models
steps.code:
from sqlalchemy import ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship

class Post(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'posts'
    id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(primary_key=True)
    title: Mapped[str]
    author_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(ForeignKey('users.id'))
    author: Mapped['User'] = relationship(back_populates='posts')

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'
    id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(primary_key=True)
    name: Mapped[str]
    posts: Mapped[list['Post']] = relationship(back_populates='author')

steps.label: Query with join
steps.code:
with Session(engine) as session:
    user = session.get(User, 1)
    print(user.posts)  # SQLAlchemy lazy-loads the posts
